author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Designing is usually done from the picture in my mind at the beginning of a project. Too often the standard boring design is chosen when so much of your own personality can be imprinted on the finished product. Like a bookshelf I am going to make that has a ribbed finish with very old hardwood mixed with new timbers.
Btw. How's the lawn mower to electric cart going?

text: Hi  Rob did I miss your final video on the DR Who lawn mower? Same was true for the green scooter and Wind generator with microwave plate turn coils. Being a great fan of your work, I never get to see the completed solution. Is this due to you showing the conclusion on the members channel? Many thanks for the countless hours you have put in.

author: @jimmycorkhill1390
text: Im seeing style over function, too many gimmicks in this era in my humble opinion. Im looking for stuff that works well and delivers on expectation in an efficient and sustainable manner. The best in design has this at heart, the style followed the function and became fashionable not the other way around. Best wishes.

author: @binmanblog
text: I think all 3 have merit but you can't design for everyone because people have different tastes and expectations, even before they see the first image appears. I hope Luke has the confidence to "do his own thing" even when based on a range of other peoples impressions of a subject. Fascinating insight and talkie bit.👌

I don't know if it falls into "Steam Punk" territory but some of those period coffee machines are the epitome of decadence!

author: @leefoster4133
text: Well, I have been dreaming of this nightmarish MPPT charge controller lately.  I have no idea why and the use case for this is what I would classified as limited.  Meaning it wouldn't be of use to 85 percent of the market.  It handles three main power rails (12V, 24V and 48V).  Each rail can send it's received power to the other rails.  Also each rail can have 3 battery system connections for that voltage with the fourth on all rails going to the 48V one for grid tie so the power can be sent back to the grid.  The over all of it is that no incoming power is wastes but it's sent somewhere to be use.  I don't know how this got in my nut but I do understand the schematics I was seeing.  It got a bit fussy around the timing circuits as you don't want a fire in the bloody thing.  I just don't know why am seeing it in my dreams.  Who would buy such a thing.  It would have to weight somewhere around 50+ kilos.  If it was needed it would have to be a massive solar arrays on all of those voltages and likely wind as well.  I wouldn't want to think about the batteries, combiner boxes, inverters and overall wiring of the blood thing.  The only thing I know is the limit for all of the voltages is 60A.  Maybe now I  have wrote it; maybe the dreams will stop.

replies.author: @MichaelDobbins
replies.text: I have a wind controller I just build with 4 inputs 12v and 48v each with 3 phase AC and DC. It has a 48v DC out to my house storage system, a 48v super capacitor and Li Battery combination. I am still building 12v and 48V wind turbines for the 3 phase inputs (which I tested with a wind generator powered by a hand drill).  I have used the 12v and 48v DC inputs to test the wind controller subsystems as well as charge/discharge the house super capacitor when working on that.  I think the idea is great.

replies.author: @leefoster4133
replies.text: @MichaelDobbins Well mate, that was in my second dream the day after when I woke the first one saying  that was the most stupid idea I have every had.  You see it's not if it is a good idea or bad idea.  It's what I call practical idea.  Yes, it may work in your use case and a few others.  And really that's what I think it is for.  It's for people that have large solar arrays, a wind farm and maybe hydro-electric.  Where all of that power goes in it and and is stored in whatever the storage is.  Lead Acid, Lithium, super caps, Nickel Iron and so-on to include sending what it couldn't use at the time to the grid where nothing is lost.  What I didn't mentioned was everything is tracked, monitored and trended via a web interface to allow the user to download the collected data to a file for long term analysis.  The overall principle is to move power where you need it.  If I have a wind mill off a 24V rail for example.  And it doesn't have a need for it at the time.  It could be send to the 48V rail or the 12V rail.  It's just a simple booster converter really.  Then that power rail will do with it how ever it needs to.  Yes, it could have been 24V @ 3A but if it goes up then it's 48V @ 1.5 A or  down to 12V @ 6A.  There would be resistance loss with it but it goes somewhere that needs it.  My problem with it is that I don't see it as commercially viable. Not all good ideas are what I call practical ones.  If I mention it to my partner I would be designing that device and he would try to find some market for it or try.  It's much like the other devices I have dreamt up.  Just wasn't practical.  I'll think about it.  I was going to create a improved 12V one anyhow.

replies.author: @MichaelDobbins
replies.text: @leefoster4133 :) yes, well put.  As an experimenter, my design evolved through several projects.  starting with an exercise bike generator, a few solar panels with individual grid tie micro inverters, a couple of different voltage sized wind generators that I created circuitry but have not yet completed the working blades part.  Circuits to help me charge and discharge the supper capacitor bank to match voltages with the house Solar and battery before connecting.  I pulled that all apart and used the parts to created the current version keeping only the generators that I think I can complete and actually use.  No mass market here. :)

author: @edkoetsier339
text: Rob, It's because human beings a pattern recognition creatures. It the way our brains have evolved, from the neural networks on the surface of our brains interlinked to the cerebellum where long term memories are are stored. And it's why, when we see something completely removed from our experiences, we may not see it at all. And it's on the human brains design that that deep learning artificial intelligence neural networks are modelled. Truly original thought is exceedingly rare. Only people like Einstein have TRULY original thought. Maybe you as well...
